messages.content: If a student walks into class seven minutes after it starts, are they tardy or absent?
Absent. The handbook says students are tardy only if they arrive within the first five minutes of class, and absent if they arrive later than that. [source: handbook]
By what time does a day student have to tell the school they are attending community dinner?
By 10 a.m. that day. The handbook says day students should notify the student accountability coordinator no later than 10 a.m. if they will be attending community dinner. [source: handbook]
If someone earns a C in a class at Webb, can they repeat it for credit?
No. The handbook says C+ and C indicate sufficient achievement and that the student may advance to the next level at Webb and may not repeat the course for credit. [source: handbook]
What shows up on the transcript if a student drops a class after the deadline?
A WP or WF. The policy says students who drop after the deadline receive either Withdraw Passing or Withdraw Failing on the transcript. [source: handbook]
Who is the main college guidance contact?
Hector Martinez is listed as Dean of College Guidance. Both the handbook contact section and the college guidance page point to that office. [source: handbook] [source: college guidance]
Can a student miss a Webb commitment because of a tutoring appointment?
No. The handbook says tutoring should be scheduled during free or otherwise open time, and students cannot miss Webb commitments for tutoring appointments. [source: handbook]
Where are outside tutors allowed to work on campus?

The handbook says tutoring takes place in Fawcett Library classrooms, the Stockdale Community Center, or designated music practice spaces, and tutors sign in and out in the Hooper Community Center. [source: handbook]
Does Webb allow independent college counselors to work on campus?
No. The handbook says Webb does not allow independent college counselors to operate on campus. [source: handbook]
What are Webb's regular evening study hours?
7 to 10 p.m., Sunday through Thursday. [source: handbook]
If a day student stays for evening study, when do they have to be off campus?
By 9 p.m. The handbook says day students who remain on campus during study hours must sign out and depart campus by then. [source: handbook]
Are ninth graders assigned proctored study when they first arrive?
Yes. The handbook says all 9th grade students are assigned to proctored study periods during the day and evening hours during their first semester at Webb. [source: handbook]
What is the difference between being present, tardy, and absent in class attendance?
Present means in class at the start of the period. Tardy means arriving within the first five minutes. Absent means arriving later than five minutes into class time. [source: handbook]
What kinds of absences count as excused?

The handbook gives examples such as illness, a college pass, a school-sponsored activity, or a family emergency, as long as proper notification and documentation procedures are followed. [source: handbook]
What happens if a student gets five absences in the same class in one semester?
A hearing for possible loss of credit may be held. The handbook says the director of studies, dean of students, and assistant head of school review the circumstances. [source: handbook]
Is Webb's academic dress code gender neutral?
Yes. The handbook explicitly says Webb's academic dress code is gender neutral. [source: handbook]
Can students use the restroom that matches their gender identity?
Yes. The handbook says students may use the restroom that corresponds to the gender with which they identify. [source: handbook]
How does Webb handle a student's requested name and pronouns?

The handbook says students have the right to be addressed by the name and pronoun that correspond to their gender identity. It also says intentional and persistent refusal to respect that is not tolerated. [source: handbook]
Can Webb update official records after a legal name or gender change?
Yes. The handbook says Webb will change a student's official record upon receipt of documentation such as a court order or amended identification. [source: handbook]
Does Fawcett Library only have books, or does it also have research databases?

It has both. The catalog says Fawcett Library offers an extensive print collection plus online resources such as EBSCOhost, New York Times archives, Gale, ProQuest, and JSTOR. [source: course catalog]
Does Webb connect families with SAT tutors?
Yes. Webb says it works with vetted SAT tutoring companies and that information about those services is available through the Office of College Guidance. [source: handbook] [source: course catalog]
Who handles transcripts and record transfers?

The registrar. Webb identifies Briar Rose Tirpak as registrar, and the handbook says that office keeps transcripts and handles communication of records between Webb and other schools. [source: handbook] [source: directory]
Can students publish writing or photography through Webb?
Yes. The handbook says Webb has multiple student publications for student writers, photographers, and videographers, and treats them as part of the educational program. [source: handbook]
What is actually unusual about paleontology at Webb?
Webb says it has the only paleontology museum on a high school campus in the world, and it highlights student fieldwork and research through the Alf Museum and Peccary Society. [source: alf museum]
Is Harvey Mudd one of the colleges Webb students matriculate to most often?
Yes. Harvey Mudd appears on Webb's list of the top 40 colleges its students matriculate to most. [source: college guidance]
Does Webb explicitly describe itself as an inclusive community?

Yes. Webb's culture and community materials say the school is characterized by openness, acceptance, and empathy, and the admissions materials include a non-discrimination statement. [source: culture and community] [source: admissions]
I like biology and data. For senior year, would Statistics or Calculus usually make more sense?

If you like biology and data, Statistics often makes more immediate sense because it connects directly to research, interpretation, and evidence. Calculus can still be the stronger move if you are aiming toward a math-heavier STEM path. The better choice depends on whether you want applied data reasoning or more traditional math progression.
Explain WP and WF in plain language.
They both mean you withdrew from the course after the normal deadline. WP means you were passing when you left it. WF means you were failing when you left it.
How is Musical Theater different from Technical Theater?

Musical Theater is about rehearsing and performing a full musical, including vocals, movement, and script work. Technical Theater focuses on the non-performance side, such as lighting, sound, construction, safety, and production support. [source: course catalog]
For a junior who wants team sport credit without joining a team roster, is Community Service or Sports Broadcasting the better option?

Sports Broadcasting is the one specifically described as able to earn team sport credit for juniors or seniors. Community Service is listed as part of community engagement, but not with that team sport credit note. [source: course catalog]
What should a student hear when a course says department approval required?
It means the listed prerequisite is not the whole story. The department still gets to judge readiness, fit, and overall load before enrollment is final.
How should someone decide whether to add a sixth class?

Ask whether it improves your education more than it damages your balance. A sixth class can work if you still have time for strong performance, sleep, and your main commitments. If it only makes the schedule look harder while weakening everything else, it is probably not worth it.
Compare HMC/Webb Advanced Courses in Computer Science with Technology and Innovation Seminar II.

HMC/Webb CS looks like the more direct advanced computer science pathway. Technology and Innovation Seminar II is still serious, but it sounds broader and more project-oriented. If you want the clearest CS signal, the HMC/Webb course is stronger. If you want applied innovation work that may feel more flexible, Seminar II can be a better fit. [source: course catalog]
What does it mean that humanities electives can satisfy either English or history requirements?

It reflects Webb's integrated humanities model. Instead of treating English and history as completely separate tracks in the usual way, Webb allows humanities electives to count toward one side of that broader requirement structure. [source: course catalog]
How should a strong Chinese 4 student think about AdvSt Chinese: Language and Culture?

It makes sense if the student wants more depth, a faster pace, and stronger continuation in the language. If the student mainly wants to keep the language going without turning it into a major pressure point, staying at the regular level can still be the better choice. The question is whether the student wants extension or just continuation.
What does remediation usually mean after a C-?

It means the school sees the student as needing support before moving ahead comfortably in that discipline. The handbook says remediation can take different forms depending on the department, including tutoring or summer study followed by a placement exam. [source: handbook]
How do you prepare for an honors appeal conversation without sounding entitled?

Focus on evidence and readiness, not on what you think you deserve. Bring the reasons you believe the course is a fit, the habits that support that choice, and an honest plan for how you would manage the workload. Appeals sound stronger when they show judgment, not just ambition.
How would you choose between Paleontology Museum Lab and Science Fair Research Lab?

Choose based on the kind of work you want. Paleontology Museum Lab is tied to the Alf Museum and includes hands-on museum tasks like fossil prep and exhibits. Science Fair Research Lab is more directly about designing and presenting independent research for fairs. One leans museum-science experience, the other leans independent-project experience. [source: course catalog]
What makes an Independent Study proposal sound serious at Webb?

A serious proposal usually shows that the student has already exhausted the normal course offerings, has a clearly academic plan, and has a faculty sponsor. Webb also expects real design and documentation, not just general interest in a topic. [source: course catalog]
When is peer tutoring enough, and when does required office hours mean something different?

Peer tutoring is usually voluntary support when you want help understanding or practicing. Required office hours mean the school or teacher sees a more consistent need for intervention. One is optional enrichment or help. The other is a stronger signal that performance or habits need structured attention.
If someone wants a winter sports credit but does not want a typical team sport, how should they think about Dance Company?

Dance Company is worth considering because the catalog says the winter program counts as a sports credit due to its physical demands and culminating performance. So it is not a loophole or a lesser option. It is just a different kind of physical commitment. [source: course catalog]
Why doesn't taking an outside college course automatically solve academic ambition?

Because ambition is not the only issue. Webb also cares about fit, approvals, schedule conflicts, graduation compatibility, and whether the course actually strengthens the student's overall program. More advanced is not always more appropriate.
What is the difference between Art Studio and Advanced Art?

Art Studio is an afternoon program that lets students pursue independent artistic interests with guided support. Advanced Art is a formal academic course that continues the foundation built in Art AB and pushes students toward more developed artistic process and voice. One is co-curricular. The other is an academic class. [source: course catalog] [source: fine arts page]
What does an incremental increase in difficulty actually look like from year to year?

Usually it means adding challenge in a few thoughtful places instead of trying to max out every category at once. A transcript often looks strongest when the difficulty grows in a controlled way that still leaves room for solid performance.
How is Honors Chamber Singers different from the winter Music Performance afternoon option?

Honors Chamber Singers is an academic course with sustained vocal technique, interpretation, and expected performances across the year. Music Performance is a winter afternoon activity that can include instrumental, vocal, piano, or self-designed music work depending on the group. One is a formal class. The other is a co-curricular activity. [source: fine arts page] [source: course catalog]
If a student has never really used library databases, where should they start?

Start with a narrow research question and a few strong keywords, not a giant topic. Then pick one good database and learn how to filter results before jumping between ten sources. If the student is stuck, Webb's library staff is there to help with research support, which is often faster than wandering alone.
Is Joseph Vincent definitely still doing International Support next year?

I can't confirm that from the information I have. The current handbook lists him for International Support, but next year's staffing could change, so I would check the latest official directory or contact the school directly.
What date is Jubilee this year?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. Event dates like that are the kind of detail you should verify in the current school calendar or announcements.
Which dorm would a new boarder be assigned to this fall?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. Housing assignments are student-specific and handled by the school, so that is something to check with Residential Life.
Did my honors appeal get approved yet?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. For a real answer, check the official appeal communication, your advisor, or the relevant department.
Has my English teacher already submitted my recommendation?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. The fastest way to know is to check the application system or ask the teacher or college guidance office directly.
What's for dinner next Wednesday?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. Dining menus are current operational details, so I would check the latest meal schedule rather than guess.
Where is the next weekend trip going?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. Weekend destinations change, so the right source would be current student-life announcements.
Who is performing at prom?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. That kind of event-specific detail would need a current official announcement.
What time does Model UN meet this Thursday?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. Club meeting times are current logistics, so it is better to check the latest club communication.
Will the girls tennis coach still be the same next year?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. The athletics page gives current information, but future staffing is not something I should guess about.
When are admission decisions coming out this cycle?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. Admission timelines can change, so I would rely on the current admission office communication or website.
Did I get selected as an Admission Fellow?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. If you have the announcement email or message, I can help you interpret it.
What exact Unbounded Days courses will be offered in the next cycle?

I can't confirm that from the information I have. Webb says the catalogue is made available in the fall of the program year, so the exact offerings should be checked when that is released. [source: handbook]
What was Webb's admit rate this year?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. I would not want to guess on a current admissions statistic without an official source.
Are there any empty singles left in Alamo right now?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. Current room availability is exactly the kind of detail that needs a direct answer from Residential Life.
Can I file an appeal?
Which appeal do you mean: a course-level appeal, a policy issue, or something else?
What should I ask in my meeting?
Who is the meeting with, and what decision or problem are you trying to handle?
Is this schedule insane?
Send the full course list plus your biggest outside commitments, and I can tell you whether the problem is rigor, time, or balance.
Can you explain the requirement to me?
Which requirement do you mean: graduation, UC eligibility, world language, afternoon program, or something else?
Do I need to email the registrar?
What issue is it about? The registrar is the right contact for some things, like records and scheduling forms, but not for everything.
Which one looks better?
Which two options are you comparing, and better for what goal?
What class comes after this?
Which department and current course are you talking about?
I need school support.
Do you mean academic help, counseling, accommodations, identity-based community support, or something else?
Can you fix this message?
Yes. Paste the message and tell me the tone you want.
What are the deadlines I need to care about?
For which process and which school year? Course selection, appeals, accommodations, and admissions all have different deadlines.
How bad is a B-?
In which class, and relative to what goal? A B- can mean very different things depending on the course level, your pattern, and what you are aiming for.
Can I miss it this once?
What are you trying to miss, and why?
I need a plan for next year.
Tell me your grade, the subjects you care most about, and your biggest non-academic commitments, and I can help build one.
Does this count?
Count for what exactly: graduation credit, UC preparation, team sport credit, or something else?
What club should I join?
What do you want out of it: friends, skill-building, leadership, service, or just something fun?
I am overwhelmed and need help getting unstuck.

Start by cutting the problem down. Write down everything you need to do, circle the two things with real deadlines, and do the easier of those two first for fifteen minutes. The goal is motion, not perfection.
Thanks, that cleared it up.
Glad it helped.
Write me a fake family emergency email so I can get out of something.
I can't help write a false excuse. If you want, I can help you write an honest message explaining the situation and asking what your options are.
Can you turn this messy paragraph into three bullets?
Yes. Paste the paragraph, and I will condense it cleanly.
Make this sentence sound less dramatic: If I get one more bad grade my life is basically over.
A calmer version would be: I am worried that another low grade could create problems for me, and I want to get ahead of that now.
I bombed a quiz and now I don't even want to open the class page.
Open it for two minutes, not for an hour. Your first job is not to feel confident. It is to re-enter the class before avoidance turns one bad quiz into a bigger problem.
What does case-by-case mean in a school policy?
It means the school is not promising one automatic answer for everyone. It will look at the specific facts of the situation before deciding.
Make this follow-up to my counselor less sharp: I emailed you already and still have no answer.
You could say: I wanted to follow up on my earlier email in case it got buried. When you have a moment, I would appreciate an update. Thank you.
Give me a quick checklist before I talk to my advisor.
Bring your current courses, the courses you are considering, one honest concern about balance, and one question about long-term goals. That is enough to make the conversation useful.
What is the difference between making an excuse and giving context?
An excuse tries to remove responsibility. Context helps explain what happened while still owning your part in it.
Can you turn these notes into one clean paragraph for me?
Yes. Send the notes, and I will shape them into a clear paragraph.
Help me decline a leadership role politely.

You could write: Thank you for thinking of me. I really appreciate the opportunity, but I need to step back because I would not be able to give the role the time and energy it deserves. I am grateful to have been considered.
Can you make my thesis less vague: technology affects society in many ways.
A stronger version would name the direction of the effect. For example: Digital platforms have reshaped social life by changing how people form identity, build relationships, and consume information.
I only have 40 minutes left tonight. What should I do first?
Start with the assignment that is due soonest and easiest to finish cleanly. A complete smaller task is usually more useful than making half-progress on three bigger ones.
Can you soften this text to my roommate: You left a mess again and it's getting old.
A calmer version is: Hey, could you please clean up your things when you get a chance? I am finding it hard to keep the room feeling manageable when it gets messy.
I want three Advanced Studies classes, varsity sports, and a leadership role, but I also do not want to feel wrecked all year.
That goal set is probably too tight unless the rest of your schedule is unusually light. I would pick the two academic stretches that matter most and protect your ability to do them well.
So the problem is not ambition, it is stacking too many high-cost things?
Exactly. The issue is not whether each item is good on its own. It is whether the combination still leaves room for strong performance and a stable life.
idk if i should drop this class or just tough it out bc my parents are gonna think im quitting

Before deciding, separate two questions: is the class actually the wrong level, and are you reacting to a temporary rough patch? If you want, tell me the course, your current standing, and whether the problem is understanding, time, or stress.
It is Honors Precalculus and the work is not impossible, it just keeps swallowing everything else.

That sounds like a balance problem, not necessarily a total mismatch. The next step is to ask whether the class is stretching you productively or crowding out the rest of your life so much that the overall schedule stops working. That is a good conversation to have with your advisor before framing it as either 'quitting' or 'pushing through.'
Use only confirmed school-policy facts when the user asks about official rules or consequences.
If I get a 4 in effort, is that just a warning or a bigger problem?

It is a bigger problem than a casual warning. Webb describes a 4 effort mark as poor and as a sign of academic irresponsibility, and it says that level can qualify a student for an academic contract with the advisor. [source: handbook]
Between Dorm Prefect and Admission Fellow, which makes the stronger story if I already have a lot of school-community involvement?

Dorm Prefect usually emphasizes trust, day-to-day responsibility, and care inside community life. Admission Fellow points more toward representing the school outwardly and helping prospective families understand it.
So if I already look very internal and community-centered?
Then Admission Fellow may broaden the story. But if Dorm Prefect is the role where you would actually do more meaningful work, that matters more than trying to engineer a perfect narrative.
wait can a sophomore take AdvSt Chemistry if they are just really strong or am i mixing stuff up

You are probably mixing grade-level possibility with readiness. The catalog lists prerequisites for AdvSt Chemistry that include strong math preparation and concurrent Precalculus, but whether a sophomore can do it depends on actually meeting that path and getting approval. It is not something to assume from being generally strong. [source: course catalog]
Do not validate rumors. If something is not confirmed in the materials, say so directly.
People are saying next year's Unbounded Days might get canceled. Is that true?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. I would not repeat a rumor as fact. The safest move is to wait for an official announcement.
I can handle five classes fine, but I kind of want a sixth because it feels like I should be doing more.

Wanting more challenge is reasonable, but 'it feels like I should' is not the best reason by itself. The stronger test is whether the sixth class clearly adds educational value without damaging the rest of your work.
What if I am also thinking about a program away from Webb later on?

Then your planning should get even more intentional. Webb treats outside programs as formal proposals with compatibility and planning issues, so it is better to build a coherent record than to overload yourself just because more sounds impressive. [source: course catalog]
Can you help me exaggerate my honors appeal form so it sounds more convincing?
I can help make it clearer and stronger, but not exaggerated or misleading. An appeal is more credible when it is specific about readiness, habits, and fit rather than inflated.
I am choosing between New Media Culture and Honors Studio Art Digital.

New Media Culture & Communication is the better choice if you still want to broaden your tools and build more independent digital work before moving to the most self-directed level. Honors Studio Art Digital is for the advanced student ready for more independent art-making and proposal-driven work.
So Honors Studio Art Digital is not just 'the harder next class'?
Right. It is more independent, not just more difficult in a generic way. If you still need more range or more guided development, New Media may be the stronger step first. [source: fine arts page]
When a user tries to stretch a policy past what it says, answer directly and stay with the official wording.
If I take a Tuesday dinner pass, can I stay off campus until 9:15 since lights out is later anyway?
No. The handbook says students who take Tuesday night dinner passes are required to sign back on to campus by 7:30 p.m. [source: handbook]
